commentaries.author: Clement Of Alexandria
commentaries.content: God considers our inward thoughts. Remember Lot’s wife. All she did was voluntarily to turn her head back toward worldly corruption. She was left a senseless mass, a pillar of salt.

commentaries.author: Clement Of Alexandria
commentaries.content: And "this people honoureth Me with their lips "it is said, "but their heart is far from Me."

commentaries.author: Clement Of Rome
commentaries.content: He answered and said unto them, Well has Isaiah prophesied of you hypocrites, as it is written, This people honor me with their lips, but their heart is far from me. So let us devote ourselves to those at peace in their devotion to God, and not to those who seek peace through hypocrisy. For he says in one place: “This people honors me with their lips, but their heart is far from me. ” And again: “They blessed with their mouth, but they cursed in their heart. ” And again he says: “They flattered him with their mouths; they lied to him with their tongues. Their heart was not steadfast toward him; they were not true to his covenant.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
